title: Diplacorchis angolensis
taxonomy: Diplacorchis angolensis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it is placed within the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Vandeae. Furthermore, within the tribe Vandeae, it is classified as part of the subtribe Angraecinae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other orchids. Further research may be necessary to confirm its exact taxonomic position within the orchid family.
uses: Diplacorchis angolensis is reported to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat various health conditions, including fever and rheumatism. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Diplacorchis angolensis for medicinal purposes.
